notes: Battle of Chandawar. Mu'izz ad-Din Muhammad, also known as Muhammad Ghori, was the Sultan of the Ghurid Empire. First battle of Tarain (1191) - Prithviraj Chauhan's Rajput forces defeat Muhammad Ghori's Ghurid army. Second battle of Tarain (1192) - Mohammad Ghori defeated Prithviraj Chauhan. Battle of Anhilwara (1178) - Mohammad Ghori was defeated by Gujarat king Mularaja II. Q.275. In the 11th century, under Maḥmud of Ghazni, Ghazni, a small town in ______, became the capital of the vast empire of the Ghaznavids. SSC CHSL 08/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) Egypt (b) Persia (c) Afghanistan (d) Turkey Sol.275.(c) Afghanistan. Mahmud of Ghazni was a Turkish conqueror who attacked India 17 times between 1000 to 1027 AD. In 1018 he plundered the holy city of Mathura and also attacked Kannauj. In 1019 and 1029 he undertook two raids on Gangetic valley. In 1025 he attacked Somnath (a town on the coast of Kathiawar).

1398. Timur invaded India in 1398 during the reign of Nasir-ud-Din Mahmud Shah Tughluq of the Tughlaq Dynasty. After the invasion, Timur appointed Khizr Khan as the ruler of Punjab. Khizr Khan later founded the Sayyid Dynasty (1414–1451).

Mahmud Ghaznavi (998 - 1030 AD) attacked India 17 times between 1000 to 1027 AD. Al-Biruni’s Books: Kitab-ul-Hind and Tahkik-e- Hind. Q.278. When did the Mongol ruler Genghis Khan die? SSC CGL 02/12/2022 (3rd Shift) (a) 1219 (b) 1210 (c) 1235 (d) 1227 Sol.278.(d) 1227. Genghis Khan (original name Temujin) was a Mongolian warrior-ruler, one of the most famous conquerors of history, who consolidated tribes into a unified Mongolia and then extended his empire across Asia to the Adriatic Sea. His last campaign against the Western Xia.

Prithviraj III (1168-1192). He defeated Sultan Muhammad Ghori in 1191 (first Battle of Tarain), but he was defeated by Ghori in 1192 (second Battle of Tarain). Vigraharaja II {(971-998), Chahamana dynasty}. Prithviraj I {(1090 -1110), Chahamana dynasty}. Vasudeva {(73 BCE to 28 BCE), founder of Kanvas Dynasty}. Delhi Sultanate (1206 AD to 1526 AD)

Mamluk dynasty: Also known as the Slave Dynasty, founded by Qutb-ud-din Aibak, who became the first Sultan of Delhi in 1206. The dynasty ruled Delhi from 1206 to 1290. Khilji Dynasty: The second dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate, ruling from 1290 to 1320. Tughlaq Dynasty: The third dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate, ruling from 1320 to 1414. Lodi Dynasty: The last dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate, ruling from 1451 to 1526.

Morocco. Ibn Battuta visited India in during the reign of Muhammad bin Tughlaq, who appointed him as the qazi (judge) of Delhi. Other notable foreign travelers : Hiuen Tsang (China) - Harshavardhana's court; Al-Beruni (Persia) - Came with Mahmud of Ghazni; Francois Bernier (France) - Shahjahan and Aurangzeb's court. Q.282. In reference to the Delhi Sultanate, what was the ‘Group of Forty’? Higher Secondary 25/06/2024 (Shift - 2) (a) They were mostly powerful Turkish slaves of Iltutmish, who took power in the interregnum following Sultana Raziyya’s killing. (b) They were forty military archers who distinguished themselves by their bravery in the Delhi Sultanate. (c) They were forty banjara leaders who transferred military supplies in times of war. (d) They were forty Rajput nobles who planned a coup to dethrone Qutb ud Din Aibak in 1210. Sol.282.(a) The 'Group of Forty' (Chalisa) in the Delhi Sultanate was a council of powerful Turkish slaves, formed by Iltutmish to assist in governance.

Mohammad bin Tughlaq . Vijaynagar Empire: Established - 1336 by Harihara and Bukka. Capital - Hampi. The Empire stretched from Krishna river in the north to extreme south of the peninsula. Dynasties with first and last rulers - Sangama Dynasty (Harihara I, Rajasekhara), Saluva Dynasty (Saluva Narasimha, Sadasiva), Tuluva Dynasty (Rama, Venkata), Aravidu Dynasty (Tirumala Raya, Sriranga III).

Iqtadari System:- Introduced by Shamsuddin IIltutmish (3rd Sultan of Delhi). Provision - Under this system, the land of the empire was divided into several large and small tracts called Iqta and assigned these Iqtas to his soldiers, officers and nobles. Administrative Terms used in Sultanate Period: Dabir (Secretary), Faujdar (Commander of army), Sipahsalar (Commander), Amil (Revenue officer), Diwan (Office: the central secretariat).

Learned theologians and Jurists. Officers in Delhi Sultanate - State Level: Amil (Revenue Officer), Barid (Intelligence agents), Khwaja (Accounts Officer), Kotwal (Head of city law and order), Muhatasib (Head of municipality law and order), Muftis (Expounder of law).

Razia Sultan. In 1236, Razia Sultan (Mamluk dynasty), succeeded her father to the Sultanate of Delhi. She sent an expedition against Ranthambore to control the Rajputs. Mamluk dynasty (1206 to 1290) - Founded by Qutbuddin Aibak (Lakh Baksh). Q.287. Under the Delhi Sultanate, a religious tax ________ was imposed on the wealthy and rich Muslims in India. SSC MTS 16/06/2023 (Evening) (a) Firuz (b) Mamluk (c) Zakat (d) Kismat Sol.287.(c) Zakat. Zakat tax was abolished by Sikander lodi. Firoz Shah Tughlaq introduced four types of taxes History based on the Quran. They were Kharaj (Land tax), Khams (Tax on booty captured in war), Jizya (Imposed on non muslim), and Zakat.

"Tabaqat-i-Nasiri'' (1260) was written by Minhaj-i-Siraj in Persian language. He was a chronicler during the rule of Sultan Iltutmish. Hasan Nizami (Persian language poet) wrote Tajul-Maasir (the first official history of the Delhi Sultanate). Sufi poet Amir Khusro was the court poet of Alauddin Khilji. His famous works: Tughluq Nama, Wast-ul-Hayat, Nuh Sipihr, Ashiqa, Khamsa, Baqia-Naqia. Ziauddin Barani (Muslim political thinker of Delhi Sultanate) wrote Tarikh-i-Firozshahi and Fatwa-i-Jahandari.

Alauddin Khilji’s Generals : Hizabruddin (Zafar Khan), Almas Beg (Ulugh Khan), Malik Kafur (Taj al-Din Izz al-Dawla), Nusrat Khan (Malik Nusrat Jalesari). Muhammad bin Tughlaq (Jauna Khan): Introduced token currency.
Ananga Pala Tomar - founded Delhi in 1052. An inscription of Vikram Samvat in the Delhi Museum confirms the founding of Delhi by the Tomars.

Yahya bin Ahmad Sirhindi. Amir Khusrau (Books - ‘Tughlaq Nama’, ‘Nuh Sipihr’, ‘Khazain ul Futuh’ ). Q.292. Who was the ruler of Delhi sultanate when Ibn Batuta came to India? SSC CHSL 04/08/2021 (Afternoon) (a) Alauddin Khilji (b) Firuz Shah Tughlaq (c) Muhammad Bin Tughlaq (d) Ibrahim Lodi Sol.292.(c) Muhammad Bin Tughlaq was the ruler of the Delhi sultanate when Ibn Batuta came to India. Muhammad bin Tughluq was the Sultan of Delhi from 1324 to 1351 from the Tughlaq Dynasty. Slave Dynasty Q.293. Who was appointed as the governor of Bengal by Sultan Balban after the revolt and subsequent death of Tughril Beg? SSC MTS 05/11/2024 (1st Shift) (a) Zafar Khan (b) Bughra Khan (c) Khizr Khan (d) Mahmud Khan Sol.293.(b) Bughra Khan. After Nasir-ud-din Mahmud died in 1266, Ghiyasuddin Balban (1266-1287) ascended the throne. He was also the first muslim ruler to formulate the ‘theory of Kingship’. Balban introduced the practice of Sijda (prostration) and Paibos (kissing the feet of Monarch). Tughril Beg revolted against Balban, Sultan of Delhi, and declared himself as an independent ruler of Bengal in 1279. Q.294. Which of the following departments of Delhi Sultanate was reorganized by Balban? SSC CGL 10/09/2024 (3rd Shift) (a) Diwan-i-Ishtiaq (b) Diwan-i-Arz (c) Diwan-i-Risalat (d) Diwan-i-Mustakhraj Sol.294.(b) Diwan-i-Arz (Department of Military). Central Departments of Delhi Sultanates: ‘Diwan-i-Risalat’ - Department of Appeals, ‘Diwan-i-Bandagan’- Department of Slaves, ‘Diwan-i-Qaza-i-Mamalik’ - Department of Justice, ‘Diwan-i-Ishtiaq’ - Department of Pensions, ‘Diwan - i - Mustakhraj’- Departments of Arrears, ‘Diwan-i-Khairat’ - Department of Charity, ‘Diwan-i-Kohi’- Department of Agriculture, ‘Diwan -i-Wizarat’ - Head Wazir, ‘Diwan -i-Waqoof’ - Department of Revenue.

Balban. Ghiyasuddin Balban (Reigned : 1266–1287) was the ninth sultan of the Slave dynasty of Delhi. He belonged to the famous group of 40 Turkic slaves of Iltutmish. He was the first Muslim ruler to formulate the 'theory of kingship' similar to the 'theory of the divine right of the kings'. He established the military department “Diwan-i-Arz”.

1236 AD. Razia Sultana was the daughter of Iltutmish and served as the Sultan of Delhi from 1236 to 1240. She was married to Malik Altunia, the governor of Bathinda. She was buried in Kaithal, Haryana. She was imprisoned by Malik Ikhtiyar-ud-din Altunia in the Qila Mubarak Fort (Bathinda). The book "Razia Queen of India" was written by Rafiq Zakaria about her life. Q.297. Which of the following Delhi sultans introduced two coins, namely silver tanka and copper jital? SSC CGL 25/09/2024 (2nd Shift) (a) Qutbuddin Aibak (b) Bahlul Lodi (c) Ghiyasuddin Balban (d) Iltutmish Sol.297.(d) Iltutmish (1210 - 1236). He was a slave and son-in-law of Qutubuddin Aibak, considered the real founder of Delhi Sultanate.
Persian. 'Sijda' refers to the act of prostration or bowing down, and 'Pabos' refers to the kissing of the monarch's feet.

Balban. The policy of 'blood and iron' implied being ruthless to the enemies, use of sword, harshness and strictness and shedding blood. Ghiyas ud din Balban was the ninth sultan of the Mamluk/Slave dynasty of Delhi.
